theory.canonical_statement: ID 415 establishes the parity conservation protocol for neutral particle flux within the L. Unlike charged species (protons/electrons) which are steered via electromagnetic coupling, neutrons are influenced by the local metric curvature. The operator n ensures that the "Vibrating Code" of the vacuum does not induce non canonical decays or spin flip transitions in the atomic nuclei of the hardware or the operator's bone matrix. By applying a parity check P across the 153,600 nodes of the DAANSsphere, the system creates a "Neutronic Buffer." This prevents the accumulation of secondary radiation residue by ensuring that any virtual neutron fluctuations are instantly re absorbed into the vacuum plenum before they can interact with the operator's calcium phosphate structure. This is essential for maintaining the "Stillness Buoyancy" of the physical frame while at the TZP.
